basics.name: Jiawei Wang
basics.label: PhD Candidate
basics.summary: With a strong background in software security analysis and program optimization, my research aims to bridge the gap between static program analysis and real-world software engineering practices.

work.name: University of New South Wales (UNSW)
highlights: Research focus on Abstract Interpretation and Static Analysis.
Maintained and extended the SVF codebase, adding various advanced features.
Enhanced front-end support for multiple LLVM versions, including opaque pointer type inference.
Developed Abstract Interpretation support in SVF for precise static analysis.
Added new clients for detecting buffer overflow, nullptr dereference, and dangling pointer issues.

work.name: Baidu Inc.
highlights: Designed and implemented DAG-based deep learning model serving, supporting CPU/GPU/Arm/Distributed deployments.
Integrated with BaiduRPC, GRPC, and PaddlePaddle Inference Lib.
Developed Python/Pybind11 wrappers to balance ease of use and performance.

work.name: Baidu Inc.
work.position: Software Engineer - Elastic CTR/PaddleRec on Kubernetes
highlights: Designed end-to-end CTR training and inference workflows.
Implemented CTR DNN Training in distributed mode, managing 400M+ sparse parameters.
Developed post-training quantization for embedding tables, achieving 87.5% storage savings.
Developed elastic scheduling mechanisms for dynamically scaling CTR training pods.

publications.name: Towards Efficient Abstract Interpretation by Unleashing the Power of Selective Widening
Precise Sparse Abstract Execution via Cross-Domain Interaction

interests.name: Research Interests
keywords: Security and Reliability in AI-Generated Code

references.name: Prof. Yulei Sui
references.reference: University of New South Wales. Email: y.sui@unsw.edu.au
